pattern: Choosing the AND group first decouples the problem, and a bit-level identity collapses the two XOR groups into a single max-subset-XOR over the bits the leftover XOR leaves free.
hints: Once the AND group is fixed, the rest splits into the two XOR groups; their two XORs always XOR together to the same fixed leftover value.
For a bit set in that leftover value, exactly one XOR group carries it no matter how you split, so those bits contribute a constant; only the bits absent from the leftover can be doubled.

Enumerate which elements form the AND group, and for the remaining elements maximize a subset-XOR restricted to the bits the leftover leaves at zero; combine that doubled value, the leftover, and the AND.

edge_inputs.description: two elements split across AND and XOR
each element to its own group
values with disjoint high bits favor XOR groups

stress.description: up to 11 elements with mid-width values - brute and optimal both finish fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 11)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 255) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: n capped at 11 so the 3^n brute oracle stays tractable

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maximizeXorAndXor(self, nums):
        n = len(nums)
        best = [0]
        def rec(i, a, andb, has_b, c):
            if i == n:
                bval = andb if has_b else 0
                best[0] = max(best[0], a + bval + c)
                return
            v = nums[i]
            rec(i + 1, a ^ v, andb, has_b, c)
            rec(i + 1, a, (andb & v) if has_b else v, True, c)
            rec(i + 1, a, andb, has_b, c ^ v)
        rec(0, 0, 0, False, 0)
        return best[0]
